prompt:
Analytics-infra ticket. Our usage-billing exporter currently drives a headless notebook to pull warehouse query results and screen-scrapes the output cell — it falls over on anything bigger than a few MB and mangles NULLs into empty strings. I want a proper Go client for the Databricks SQL Statement Execution API instead: package dbsql (module go-databricks-statement-exec, stdlib only, root of the repo). dbsql_test.go is the acceptance contract and runs under -race.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Databricks documentation for the Statement Execution API — at minimum the official Statement Execution guide/reference covering the execute request (statement, warehouse_id, parameters, the wait_timeout range and 0s asynchronous mode, on_wait_timeout, disposition INLINE vs EXTERNAL_LINKS and their size caps, format), the status/polling contract and its state machine, the manifest/result shapes for both dispositions, and how presigned external links must be fetched (pay attention to what the docs say about the Authorization header there — that detail is a hard test). Only first-party Databricks pages count as sources (docs.databricks.com, official Databricks SDK docs, or Databricks' own SDK repos). Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both protected. If the live docs disagree with the fixtures, flag the conflict in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Surface the tests compile against:
- New(Config{BaseURL, Token, HTTPClient *http.Client (nil => http.DefaultClient), Sleep func(time.Duration), PollInterval time.Duration}) (*Client, error) — reject empty BaseURL/Token. Never time.Sleep; all waiting goes through the injected Sleep.
- Param{Name, Value, Type string}; ExecRequest{Statement, WarehouseID, Catalog, Schema string, Parameters []Param, WaitTimeout, OnWaitTimeout, Disposition, Format string, RowLimit int64}.
- (*Client).Exec(ctx, ExecRequest) (*Result, error): POST the documented execute operation with Bearer auth, Accept and Content-Type application/json, the request fields under their documented wire names, and parameters as documented {name, value, type} objects in order. If the response state is non-terminal, poll the documented statement GET, sleeping PollInterval between polls, honoring ctx cancellation (the returned error must wrap context.Canceled).
- Result{StatementID string, Columns []Column, Rows [][]*string, TotalRowCount int64, ChunkCount int, Truncated bool}; Column{Name, TypeName, TypeText string, Position int} — manifest schema and counts retained verbatim; JSON_ARRAY values stay string-encoded with SQL NULL as nil.
- EXTERNAL_LINKS: download every external_link (they're presigned — follow the docs on auth headers exactly), follow each next_chunk_internal_link on the API host with Bearer auth until exhausted, and assemble rows in order. Enforce contiguity: a chunk whose row_offset doesn't equal the rows already assembled is an error identifying the chunk. Each link is fetched exactly once.
- Failures: a terminal FAILED statement returns *StatementError{StatementID, ErrorCode, SQLState, Message} decoded from the documented status error shape; non-2xx API responses return *APIError{StatusCode, ErrorCode, Message} from the standard Databricks error envelope. No error string may contain the token or an Authorization value.

Acceptance: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... — it runs a loopback fake workspace plus a separate loopback presigned-link host; no real Databricks, no credentials. dbsql_test.go and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

official_sources.title: Statement Execution API: Run SQL on warehouses | Databricks on AWS
official_sources.used_for: base path /api/2.0/sql/statements plus poll GET /{id}, chunk GET /{id}/result/chunks/{index}, cancel POST /{id}/cancel; request fields statement/warehouse_id/catalog/schema/parameters{name,value,type}/wait_timeout (5s-50s, default 10s, 0s async)/on_wait_timeout CONTINUE|CANCEL/disposition INLINE (25 MiB cap) vs EXTERNAL_LINKS (100 GiB cap)/format JSON_ARRAY|ARROW_STREAM|CSV/row_limit/byte_limit; manifest schema.columns {name,type_name,type_text,position}, total_chunk_count/total_row_count/truncated, chunks {chunk_index,row_offset,row_count}; result data_array vs external_links {chunk_index, external_link, expiration, next_chunk_internal_link, next_chunk_index}; Bearer auth; the explicit warning to OMIT the Authorization header when downloading presigned external links

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java StatementStatus.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: status envelope fields: state enum PENDING/RUNNING/SUCCEEDED/FAILED/CANCELED/CLOSED, error (ServiceError), sql_state (SQLSTATE code on failure)

research.language_rationale: The warehouse tooling this exporter joins is written in Go. Databricks documents the Statement Execution API as a plain REST/JSON surface and its official Go SDK is a thin generated wrapper over these same endpoints, so stdlib net/http with encoding/json against the documented paths is the credible zero-dependency integration surface; JSON_ARRAY string-encoded values map naturally onto Go's *string for NULL-preserving typed values.
